commit.message:
fix(db): use SECURITY DEFINER helper for Storage upload policy

The anon role cannot query devices table (RLS blocks it), so the
EXISTS subquery always returns false. Using a SECURITY DEFINER
function bypasses RLS, matching the pattern of all other device RPCs.

commit.message:
Implement T-074: Agent aging timers and life stage transitions

- Create AgingSystem class to manage agent lifespans
- Tracks aging counters for each registered agent
- RegisterAgent(agent) initializes counter based on life stage
- AdvanceCycle() decrements counters and handles transitions
Death (10)
- Elder death: Sets HP to 0 and unregisters agent
- Returns list of agents that transitioned each cycle
- GetRemainingCycles(agent) returns cycles until next transition
- GetRegisteredAgentCount() and GetRegisteredAgents() for tracking
- Clear() to reset system
- Add 21 comprehensive NUnit tests in AgingSystemTests
- Tests cover initialization, transitions, full life progression
- Tests cover multi-agent independent counters, death handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix playback race at engine level with load-generation counter

Add loadGen/consumedGen counters to the C++ engine so stale
playback_stop IPC calls (from fire-and-forget stops) cannot clear
sessionPath between a newer playback_load and start_output_stream.

- playbackLoad: ++loadGen before setting sessionPath
- startOutputStreamLocked: consumedGen = loadGen when start_playback
- playbackStopLocked: only clear sessionPath when loadGen == consumedGen

This eliminates the race without serializing stop/start IPC calls,
removing the ~200-500ms latency the JS-level drain introduced.

Revert JS drain: remove _pendingEngineStop await from
enginePlaybackStart and restore void fire-and-forget at all 3
stop call sites (video.js, audio.js×2).

commit.message:
fix: replace hard weight clip with soft cap + monotonicity guard

The prescription system hard-clipped weights to the historical max
(get_max_recent_entered_weight), which prevented progressive overload
and produced absurd prescriptions when the curve solved for heavier
weights (e.g., 200 lb x 14 reps @ RPE 9 after doing 200 lb x 13 @ RPE 8).

Changes:
- prescribe_next_set: soft cap at 125% of max(historical, session) weight
- prescribe_next_set: monotonicity guard bumps weight if clipping would
  produce more reps at same weight with higher RPE than previous set
- plan_progressive_sets: same soft cap + monotonicity guard treatment
- detect_inflection: unchanged (already used 125% soft cap)

Tests:
- Updated test_max_weight_soft_cap (was test_max_weight_clipping)
- Added test_monotonicity_guard_prevents_absurd_prescription
- Added test_prescribe_set3_respects_monotonicity (API endpoint)

Fix Chromium video freeze on workspace switch

The root cause is Hyprland stopping frame callbacks for windows on
invisible workspaces, which starves Chromium's video decode pipeline
on NVIDIA Wayland. Fix by adding a render_unfocused window rule for
Chromium and raising render_unfocused_fps from 15 to 60.

Revert the ineffective Chromium backgrounding flags from the previous
attempt — the issue is compositor-side, not browser-side.

commit.message:
Fix video spinner hiding before playback starts in engine path

_wireVideoPlayerLoadingListeners hides the spinner on <video> loadeddata/
canplay events, but when using the AudioEngine path the muted <video>
element finishes loading well before enginePlaybackStart resolves. The
spinner vanished while the picture stayed frozen until vid.play() ran.

Bump _videoPlayerLoadUiSeq to invalidate the premature <video>-event
hide callbacks, then hide the spinner manually after vid.play() in both
the engine-success and engine-fallback branches.

Promote canonical consent & structured intake (#97)

* Promote canonical consent & structured intake

Add canonical IntakeConsentPacket to draft and UI state and wire it through create/save/submit flows. MedicalHistoryCard and review/demographics steps now use server-backed supplemental intake catalogs and structured-data IDs (medications, comorbidities, assistive devices, living situations, house layouts) while syncing legacy label sets for compatibility. IntakeApiService now includes the typed consent packet in payloads; IntakeWizardPage builds/clones consent packets, evaluates completeness, and maps revoked consent keys. Helpers were added to sync legacy selections, clone/build consent packets, and resolve catalog labels. Tests and CHANGELOG updated to cover the new canonical intake UI slice.
